tranche_title: Mediation Contract Preflight And VAK Baseline
scope_summary: Inventory the current Anima/Sophia/Pi/Aletheia mediation surface, the live S5 epii-agent contract, the upstream track surfaces Track 09 will consume (Tracks 01/04/05/06/07/08), and the six operational-capacity route profiles. Every row is either backed by a live observation (CLI output, file parse, code path) or explicitly flagged as a readiness_blocker. No prose-only assumptions are recorded as ready.

readiness_status_legend.authoritative: Surface exists as live, test-locked code/CLI/file in the current repo and can be consumed today.
readiness_status_legend.transitional: Surface exists but is partial, in flux, or behind a flag; downstream tranches should code defensively.
readiness_status_legend.blocked: Surface is named in upstream plans but has no live implementation; Track 09 must either build the binding through gateway/library adapters or report blocked readiness.

privacy_gate_legend.public_current: Public-current bridge data, safe for `/body` and `/pratibimba/system` surfacing.
privacy_gate_legend.session_scoped: Carries session/DAY/NOW lineage; safe within governed session boundary; never raw-protected.
privacy_gate_legend.protected_local: Originated in user-private substrate (Nara journal/dream/oracle, Graphiti body); only safe handles and aggregate-metric derivations may cross M5-4.
privacy_gate_legend.review_gate: Must traverse an S5 review item with human-required or user-final validation gate before downstream use.
privacy_gate_legend.forbidden_to_agents: Authority reserved for human or Epii position; agents may surface and request but never resolve.

vak_baseline_for_track_09.summary: Track 09 itself is Sophia-led integration work (Anuttara, Paramasiva, Parashakti, Mahamaya, Epii-on-Epii share Sophia as governance lead; Nara is Anima-primary), while Anima remains the dispatch function across all six profiles. The baseline is recorded against the live `epi agent vak evaluate` heuristic output for representative tasks drawn from this plan body.
vak_baseline_for_track_09.nara_exception: Nara workflow is Anima-primary with five Anima gates (admission/refresh-trigger/deployment/rollback/DPO-trigger); Sophia is secondary for cross-subsystem coherence.

baseline_invocations.task_text: Anuttara: review a SHACL/reasoner construction proposal as a load-bearing axiom elaboration requiring Sophia review and user-final validation
Paramasiva: refresh CPT/RAG corpus with synthetic-proof validation and request Sophia plus Epii co-review with Pi training dispatch
Parashakti: detect embedding-quality metric drift and request graph-relational ML hot-swap with Anima cluster/rotation/Klein aesthetic check
Mahamaya: execute process-reward federated training round with tier-asymmetric review and Pi rollback handle preparation
Nara: dialogic-voice QLoRA adapter refresh trigger requires Anima admission gate, consent verification, and PII stripping before Sophia coherence review
Epii-on-Epii: recursive Sophia-on-Sophia anti-self-justification review of spine governance mechanism with user-final validation gate

vak_baseline_for_track_09.baseline_caveat: The current `epi agent vak evaluate` returns a heuristic keyword-driven mapping; five of the six representative tasks collapse to cf=(5/0)/cfp=CFP0 because the heuristic does not yet discriminate among capacity-profile vocabularies. Track 09 T1 must build a profile-aware mapping (or accept the heuristic as a deterministic baseline) so that capacity-specific route decisions are derivable from VAK rather than re-inferred from caller intent. Flagged as readiness_blocker `vak.profile-aware-mapping`.

cli_surfaces.name: epi agent vak evaluate
cli_surfaces.notes: Per Track 09 keystones: `dispatch_tools[]` carry `upstream_required: ["vak-evaluate"]`; dispatch without a real prior VAK evaluation must be rejected at the gate. Current CLI is heuristic; downstream gateway-shaped binding is Track 09 T1 work.

cli_surfaces.name: epi agent team {create|dispatch|list|resolve|stop}
cli_surfaces.notes: Durable multi-agent team substrate is live (per Track 09 Dependencies section).

cli_surfaces.name: epi gate dispatch anima-invoke
cli_surfaces.notes: Wired through gateway per D3 chip, commit 419aac5 (referenced in Track 09 plan body). Track 09 T1 must extend the binding to the full set of dispatch tools.

cli_surfaces.name: epi agent verify-runtime
cli_surfaces.notes: Smoke harness for real PI runtime; required by Track 09 T2 to prove the AgentRunEnvelope reaches a managed agent runtime when provider credentials are available.

cli_surfaces.name: epi agent anima | epi agent epii | epi agent aletheia

agent_contracts.notes: Sophia is recorded in the matrix as a constitutional agent under Anima orchestration (`role_restrictions: leaf, review_disclosure_and_epii_deposit`). Track 09 T2 must extend governance metadata for Sophia-as-governance-lead while preserving Sophia-as-Anima-constitutional-agent.

Aletheia is NOT a peer agent like Epii — it is a set of subagents that act as powers and tool guardians, already specced and mapped in the S4 layer (Body/S/S4/ta-onta/S4-5p-aletheia/CONTRACT.md). Registered tools (aletheia_gnosis_ingest/query/notebook_create, aletheia_thought_route, aletheia_crystallise) and PI-native subagents (Anansi, Moirai, Janus, Mercurius, Agora, Zeithoven) are declared there. All Aletheia invocation routes through Anima dispatch — Aletheia is emergent, not routed independently. Track 09 T2 hangs governance metadata off this S4 contract; no parallel `aletheia-agent/agent-contract.json` is required.

agent_contracts.readiness_blocker: Track 09 plan requires Sophia to be the default long-arc wisdom/governance lead across five of six capacity profiles, but the current capability matrix encodes Sophia as a constitutional leaf agent. Track 09 T2 must add M5-4 governance metadata that lifts Sophia to capacity-specific governance lead WITHOUT breaking the three-way parity test or the existing Anima-orchestrator pattern.

agent_contracts.role: bounded technical dispatcher and evidence preparer
agent_contracts.readiness_blocker: Pi is a runtime (managed PI agent directory) rather than a constitutional agent in the matrix; no separate capability JSON encodes 'Pi can prepare evidence and dispatch bounded runs but cannot approve human-required review'. Track 09 T2 must add this metadata.

track_01_bridge_capabilities.notes: Named as the single public-current profile contract in Track 01 keystones; profile JSON fixtures from real `portal-core::MathemeHarmonicProfile::from_tick` are required by Track 01 T2.
Shared TypeScript contract consumed by `/body` and `/pratibimba/system`.

track_01_bridge_capabilities.capability: VAK/CF metadata (canonical-prefix CPF/CT/CP/CF/CFP/CS keys carried end-to-end)
track_01_bridge_capabilities.notes: sessions.patch RPC `vak_address` is live (C1 chip, commit 19fbc8fc). Kernel observations, capability invocations, and review-deposit envelopes must carry the canonical-prefix keys.

track_01_bridge_capabilities.capability: Bounded agent capabilities (readCurrentProfile, readPointerAnchor, readGraphContext, subscribeRunEvents, invokeGatewayRpc, depositReviewEvidence, requestDryRunPromotion)
track_01_bridge_capabilities.owner: Track 01 T7 (Agentic Capability And Observability Feed)
track_01_bridge_capabilities.notes: Allowlist is named in Track 09 T3 but the implementation is gated on Track 01 T7. Track 09 must declare blocked rather than fake capabilities.

track_04_s5_dtos.dto: TargetSubsystem (Anuttara, Paramasiva, Parashakti, Mahamaya, Nara, Epii)
track_04_s5_dtos.notes: Closed enum derived from the six operational-capacity specs. Track 09 T4 must map capacity workflows onto this enum.

track_04_s5_dtos.dto: OrchestrationState (Routed, AwaitingArticulation, BackendSpecified, ReviewInProgress, ReviewApproved, AwaitingUserFinalValidation, IntegrationInProgress, IntegrationCompleted, Discarded, ...)

track_04_s5_dtos.notes: Per Track 04 keystone: non-dry-run execution must stay blocked until compiler mutation law, rollback capture, deployment gate, and per-target mutation contracts are implemented. Track 09 keeps surfacing dry-run plans only.

track_04_s5_dtos.dto: ReviewSource identity shape (Sophia/Pi/Epii/Nara-specific variants vs coarse + actor metadata)
track_04_s5_dtos.notes: Listed in Track 09 Open Decisions. Track 09 needs the answer for control-room filters and authority tests. Recorded here as a prose-only open decision — NOT as ready.

track_06_surface_hooks.notes: Intent envelope must carry coordinate, DAY/NOW, session key, profile generation, privacy class, source artifact handle, and target surface. No raw protected body text.

track_07_surface_hooks.hook: Spine-state inspector composition (split between Track 04 / Track 07 / Track 09)
track_07_surface_hooks.owner: Track 07 m5-epii + Track 04 S5 state + Track 09 mediation
track_07_surface_hooks.notes: Listed in Track 09 Open Decisions. Ownership of inspector composition is unresolved. Prose-only — flagged.

track_08_surface_hooks.hook: 1-2-3 plugin evidence envelopes and review requests
4/5/0 Epii review, consent, and canon-recognition hooks

six_operational_capacity_route_profiles.user_final_gate: required at tier-asymmetric thresholds for non-routine updates

six_operational_capacity_route_profiles.readiness_blocker: Nara consent/corpus storage boundary (artifact schema, revocation behavior, Anima-bound corpus partition API) is unresolved per Track 09 Open Decisions; full Nara voice mediation cannot be production-ready until it lands.

six_operational_capacity_route_profiles.user_final_gate: required for criterion or spine mechanism changes

readiness_blockers.summary: Current `epi agent vak evaluate` heuristic collapses five of six capacity-profile representative tasks to identical VAK addresses. Capacity-specific dispatch cannot derive from VAK alone today.
readiness_blockers.downstream_impact: Route decisions cannot be selected purely from VAK output; T1 must either build a profile-aware mapping or treat the heuristic as a deterministic baseline and route on additional envelope fields.

readiness_blockers.summary: Sophia is encoded as a constitutional leaf agent under Anima in the capability matrix; Track 09 plan requires Sophia to be capacity-specific governance lead. Three-way parity test must remain green after extension.
Pi is a runtime, not a capability-matrix agent. No JSON encodes 'Pi can prepare evidence and dispatch bounded runs but cannot approve human-required review'.

Bounded agent capabilities allowlist (readCurrentProfile, readPointerAnchor, readGraphContext, subscribeRunEvents, invokeGatewayRpc, depositReviewEvidence, requestDryRunPromotion) is gated on Track 01 T7.
Non-dry-run promotion blocked until Hen/S1 mutation law, rollback capture, deployment gate, and per-target MutationKind execution are implemented.
readiness_blockers.owner: Track 04 (upstream); Track 09 keeps surfacing dry-run plans only

readiness_blockers.summary: ReviewSource expansion vs. coarse + actor metadata is unresolved. Recorded here because Track 09 plan flags it as an open decision; Track 09 control-room filters and authority tests need the answer.
Theia Agentic Control Room host not yet implemented. Track 09 T9 cannot deliver acceptance without it.
Evidence Workbench pane required by Track 09 surfacing flows.
Inspector composition ownership split between Track 04 (S5 state), Track 07 (m5-epii), and Track 09 (mediation semantics) is unresolved per Open Decisions.
Nara artifact schema, revocation behavior, Anima-bound corpus partition API are unresolved per Open Decisions; Nara voice mediation cannot be production-ready until landed.

Remaining dispatch-tool variants (dispatch_parallel_agents, dispatch_fusion_agents, dispatch_moirai_night_pass, anima_self_invoke, run_chain) live in PI/ta-onta runtime; gateway/library bindings are not complete.
`pratibimba/` directory does not exist in the current repo and the smallest real `/pratibimba/system` receiver that satisfies 'not a placeholder' has no joint acceptance definition.

epi agent vak evaluate "Mahamaya: execute process-reward federated training round with tier-asymmetric review and Pi rollback handle preparation" --json

follow_up_notes: Track 09 T1 should treat the VAK heuristic as a deterministic baseline; profile-aware mapping is an open spec gap.
Track 09 T2 needs Aletheia + Sophia + Pi capability JSON parity before governance metadata is meaningful.
Track 09 T9 acceptance is blocked until Track 05 lands the Theia Agentic Control Room host.
